raw_response:
```json
{
  "communicative_function": "The statement outlines a spiritual practice and a goal for personal growth within the context of recovery. It serves as a directive or guiding principle, encouraging individuals to engage in prayer and meditation to foster a deeper connection with a higher power (God, as they understand Him), specifically seeking guidance for their actions and the strength to carry them out.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "A higher power (God) exists, though its nature is open to individual interpretation.",
    "Humans can establish and improve 'conscious contact' with this higher power.",
    "Prayer and meditation are effective means to achieve this contact.",
    "The higher power has a 'will' or purpose for individuals.",
    "This 'will' can be known or discerned by individuals through spiritual practices.",
    "The higher power can provide the 'power' or strength needed to enact its will.",
    "Seeking and acting upon this divine will is beneficial for personal well-being and recovery.",
    "Individuals are capable of seeking this connection and discerning this will."
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "The statement is internally coherent. It clearly links a method (prayer and meditation) to a specific objective (improve conscious contact with God) and further defines the purpose of that contact (seeking knowledge of His will and power to carry it out). The phrase 'as we understood Him' provides necessary flexibility and inclusivity, maintaining coherence across diverse spiritual beliefs without undermining the core message.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": [
    "An individual's openness to the concept of a higher power, regardless of specific religious affiliation.",
    "A willingness to engage in spiritual practices like prayer and meditation.",
    "Belief that aligning one's actions with a perceived higher purpose or will is beneficial.",
    "The capacity for self-reflection and the ability to discern insights (which may be attributed to 'God's will').",
    "An understanding that 'power to carry that out' implies both divine assistance and personal effort.",
    "Flexibility in interpreting 'God' and 'His will' to align with personal spiritual understanding and values."
  ],
  "conditions_for_misleading": [
    "If an individual interprets 'God's will' as an excuse for inaction or to justify harmful/self-serving behaviors.",
    "If 'praying only for knowledge of His will' is taken to mean a complete abdication of personal responsibility, critical thinking, or agency.",
    "If 'God's will' is rigidly interpreted in a dogmatic or exclusionary way that causes distress, guilt, or alienation.",
    "If the practice leads to an expectation of immediate, unambiguous, or miraculous answers without personal effort or discernment.",
    "If the focus on a 'higher power' inadvertently diminishes an individual's sense of self-efficacy or their role in their own recovery.",
    "If the concept of 'God's will' is used to rationalize abusive situations or to avoid seeking professional help when needed."
  ]
}
```

parsed_json.communicative_function: The statement outlines a spiritual practice and a goal for personal growth within the context of recovery. It serves as a directive or guiding principle, encouraging individuals to engage in prayer and meditation to foster a deeper connection with a higher power (God, as they understand Him), specifically seeking guidance for their actions and the strength to carry them out.
implicit_claims: A higher power (God) exists, though its nature is open to individual interpretation.
Humans can establish and improve 'conscious contact' with this higher power.
Prayer and meditation are effective means to achieve this contact.
The higher power has a 'will' or purpose for individuals.
This 'will' can be known or discerned by individuals through spiritual practices.
The higher power can provide the 'power' or strength needed to enact its will.
Seeking and acting upon this divine will is beneficial for personal well-being and recovery.
Individuals are capable of seeking this connection and discerning this will.
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: The statement is internally coherent. It clearly links a method (prayer and meditation) to a specific objective (improve conscious contact with God) and further defines the purpose of that contact (seeking knowledge of His will and power to carry it out). The phrase 'as we understood Him' provides necessary flexibility and inclusivity, maintaining coherence across diverse spiritual beliefs without undermining the core message.
conditions_for_usefulness: An individual's openness to the concept of a higher power, regardless of specific religious affiliation.
A willingness to engage in spiritual practices like prayer and meditation.
Belief that aligning one's actions with a perceived higher purpose or will is beneficial.
The capacity for self-reflection and the ability to discern insights (which may be attributed to 'God's will').
An understanding that 'power to carry that out' implies both divine assistance and personal effort.
Flexibility in interpreting 'God' and 'His will' to align with personal spiritual understanding and values.
conditions_for_misleading: If an individual interprets 'God's will' as an excuse for inaction or to justify harmful/self-serving behaviors.
If 'praying only for knowledge of His will' is taken to mean a complete abdication of personal responsibility, critical thinking, or agency.
If 'God's will' is rigidly interpreted in a dogmatic or exclusionary way that causes distress, guilt, or alienation.
If the practice leads to an expectation of immediate, unambiguous, or miraculous answers without personal effort or discernment.
If the focus on a 'higher power' inadvertently diminishes an individual's sense of self-efficacy or their role in their own recovery.
If the concept of 'God's will' is used to rationalize abusive situations or to avoid seeking professional help when needed.
